prerequisites: A Clarifai account (sign up at clarifai.com).
A Clarifai credential for the `api_key` field: either a Personal Access Token (PAT) or an app-specific API Key.
Your Clarifai `user_id` (the ID of the resource owner whose data you want to read).
A `start_date` to bound the data the connector begins syncing from.

authMethods.name: API key (Personal Access Token or app-specific API Key)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with a single `api_key` secret. Clarifai accepts either a Personal Access Token (PAT), which authenticates access to your own resources and resources outside the scope of a single app, or an App-Specific API Key, which is scoped to a particular Clarifai app. Both are sent in the request as the `Authorization: Key YOUR_TOKEN` header. PATs do not expire; rotate by deleting and recreating them if compromised.

setupSteps.title: Create a Clarifai account
setupSteps.body: Sign up and log in at clarifai.com. A default Personal Access Token is generated automatically when your account is created, and you can create additional tokens as needed.

setupSteps.title: Create a Personal Access Token
setupSteps.body: In the Clarifai portal, open Settings in the left sidebar and choose Secrets. Click Create Personal Access Token, enter a short description, set the scopes you want the token to have, and confirm with Create Personal Access Token. Copy the generated token; this is the value for the connector's `api_key` field. Clarifai sends it as the `Authorization: Key YOUR_PAT` header. Alternatively, use an app-specific API Key if you want access limited to a single app.

setupSteps.title: Find your user_id
setupSteps.body: Provide the `user_id` of the resource owner. In Clarifai, the `user_id` (together with `app_id`) identifies whose resources a request targets; use your own `user_id` to read your own resources.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the `api_key` (your PAT or app-specific API Key), your `user_id`, and a `start_date`. The `start_date` bounds the point in time from which the connector begins syncing data. All three fields are required.
